monadic.title: Identity
monadic.desc: Returns X unchanged.

dyadic.title: Add
dyadic.desc: Adds A and B element by element. Scalars broadcast over arrays.

monadic.title: Negate
monadic.desc: Flips the sign of every element of X.

dyadic.title: Subtract
dyadic.desc: Subtracts B from A element by element.

monadic.title: Sign
monadic.desc: Returns ¯1 for negatives, 0 for zero, 1 for positives.

dyadic.title: Multiply
dyadic.desc: Multiplies A and B element by element.

monadic.title: Reciprocal
monadic.desc: Returns 1 divided by X.

dyadic.title: Divide
dyadic.desc: Divides A by B element by element.

monadic.title: Exponential
monadic.desc: Returns e raised to X (Euler's constant).

dyadic.title: Power
dyadic.desc: Raises A to the power of B.

monadic.title: Natural log
monadic.desc: Returns the natural logarithm of X (base e).

dyadic.title: Logarithm in base A
dyadic.desc: Returns the logarithm of B in base A.

monadic.title: Ceiling
monadic.desc: Rounds X up to the nearest integer.

dyadic.title: Maximum
dyadic.desc: Returns the larger of A and B element by element.

monadic.title: Floor
monadic.desc: Rounds X down to the nearest integer.

dyadic.title: Minimum
dyadic.desc: Returns the smaller of A and B element by element.

monadic.title: Absolute value
monadic.desc: Returns the magnitude of X (negatives become positives).

dyadic.title: Residue
dyadic.desc: Returns the remainder when B is divided by A.

monadic.title: Factorial
monadic.desc: Returns N! (the product 1×2×…×N).

dyadic.title: Binomial coefficient
dyadic.desc: Number of ways to choose A items from B (B choose A).

monadic.title: Random integer
monadic.desc: Returns a uniform random integer between 1 and X.

exampleNotes.mo0: The result changes each time you run it.

monadic.title: Multiply by π
monadic.desc: Returns X multiplied by π.

dyadic.title: Circular family
dyadic.desc: A selects a circular function: 1 = sine, 2 = cosine, 3 = tangent, 0 = √(1-B²). Negative values give the inverse functions.

dyadic.title: Less than
dyadic.desc: Returns 1 where A is strictly less than B, 0 otherwise.

dyadic.title: Less or equal
dyadic.desc: Returns 1 where A is less than or equal to B.

dyadic.title: Equal
dyadic.desc: Returns 1 where A equals B, 0 otherwise. Tolerance is used for floats.

dyadic.title: Greater or equal
dyadic.desc: Returns 1 where A is greater than or equal to B.

dyadic.title: Greater than
dyadic.desc: Returns 1 where A is strictly greater than B.

dyadic.title: Not equal
dyadic.desc: Returns 1 where A differs from B, 0 where they match.

monadic.title: Depth
monadic.desc: Maximum nesting level of X. 0 for a simple scalar, 1 for a simple array, more for nested arrays.

dyadic.title: Match
dyadic.desc: Returns 1 if A and B have the same shape and matching values element by element, otherwise 0.

monadic.title: Tally
monadic.desc: Length of the first axis of X. Returns 1 for a scalar.

dyadic.title: Not match
dyadic.desc: Returns 0 if A and B match structurally, otherwise 1.

dyadic.title: Logical AND
dyadic.desc: Returns 1 only when both A and B are 1. Operates on 0/1 values.

dyadic.title: Logical OR
dyadic.desc: Returns 1 when at least one of A or B is 1.

dyadic.title: NAND
dyadic.desc: The negation of AND: returns 0 only when both inputs are 1.

dyadic.title: NOR
dyadic.desc: The negation of OR: returns 1 only when both inputs are 0.

monadic.title: Logical NOT
monadic.desc: Flips 0 to 1 and 1 to 0 over every element.

monadic.title: Shape
monadic.desc: Returns the dimensions of X as a vector (e.g. 2 3 for a 2-by-3 matrix).

dyadic.title: Reshape
dyadic.desc: Builds an array of shape A, filling it by cycling through the values of B.

monadic.title: Ravel
monadic.desc: Flattens X into a single vector, walking row by row.

dyadic.title: Catenate
dyadic.desc: Joins A and B end-to-end along the last axis.

monadic.title: Table
monadic.desc: Turns a vector into a one-column matrix.

dyadic.title: Catenate first
dyadic.desc: Stacks A on top of B along the first axis.

monadic.title: Reverse
monadic.desc: Reverses the elements along the last axis.

dyadic.title: Rotate
dyadic.desc: Shifts the elements of B by A positions to the left. Negative shifts go right.

monadic.title: Reverse first axis
monadic.desc: Reverses the elements along the first axis.

dyadic.title: Rotate first axis
dyadic.desc: Shifts the rows of B by A positions.

monadic.title: Transpose
monadic.desc: Swaps the axes of X. For a matrix, this is the usual transpose.

monadic.title: Index generator
monadic.desc: Returns the integers from 1 to N.

dyadic.title: Index of
dyadic.desc: Finds the first position of each element of B inside A. Missing elements return length(A)+1.

dyadic.title: Take
dyadic.desc: Takes A items from the start of B (or from the end if A is negative). Pads with a fill value if A is larger than B.

dyadic.title: Drop
dyadic.desc: Discards A items from B. Positive drops from the start, negative from the end.

monadic.title: Enclose
monadic.desc: Wraps X inside a scalar so it can be carried as a single item.

monadic.title: Disclose
monadic.desc: Returns the first element of X (the inverse of enclose).

dyadic.title: Member of
dyadic.desc: Returns 1 where each element of A appears anywhere in B.

dyadic.title: Find substring
dyadic.desc: Returns 1 at every starting position where A appears as a contiguous run inside B.

monadic.title: Unique
monadic.desc: Keeps each distinct value once, in the order it first appears.

dyadic.title: Union
dyadic.desc: Joins A and B keeping only one copy of each value.

dyadic.title: Intersection
dyadic.desc: Returns the values that appear in both A and B.

monadic.title: Format as text
monadic.desc: Returns the textual representation of X as a vector of characters.

monadic.title: Grade up
monadic.desc: Returns the indices that would sort X in ascending order.

monadic.title: Grade down
monadic.desc: Returns the indices that would sort X in descending order.

monadic.title: Nest
monadic.desc: Wraps X in a scalar container if it is not already a simple scalar. Useful as a guarantee that depth is at least 1.

dyadic.title: Partitioned enclose
dyadic.desc: Groups B into a nested vector. A new group starts whenever A rises to a positive value (or repeats a different positive value). Zero entries in A drop the matching item.

monadic.title: Indices of ones
monadic.desc: Returns the positions where the boolean vector X holds 1.

dyadic.title: Index along leading axes
dyadic.desc: Selects the element or slice of B sitting at index A along the leading axes.

dyadic.title: Base value
dyadic.desc: Converts digits B written in base A into a single scalar.

dyadic.title: Represent in base
dyadic.desc: Converts the scalar B into its digits in the bases listed in A.

monadic.title: Reduce along last axis
monadic.desc: F/X applies F between consecutive elements of X, right to left, along the last axis.

monadic.title: Reduce along first axis
monadic.desc: Like /, but reduces along the first axis (down each column of a matrix).

monadic.title: Scan along last axis
monadic.desc: Like reduce, but returns every running total instead of only the final one.

monadic.title: Scan along first axis
monadic.desc: Cumulative running reduction, going down each column.

monadic.title: Apply to each item
monadic.desc: F¨ X applies the function F to every item of X individually.

monadic.title: Commute / Self-apply
monadic.desc: Used monadically: F⍨ X equals X F X. Used dyadically: A F⍨ B equals B F A (it swaps the arguments).

dyadic.title: Inner product
dyadic.desc: A f.g B pairs the elements of A and B with g, then reduces with f. Use +.× for matrix multiplication.

dyadic.title: Outer product
dyadic.desc: A ∘.f B builds a table by applying f to every pair (one from A, one from B).

monadic.title: Apply N times
monadic.desc: f⍣N applies f to its argument N times in sequence. f⍣0 is the identity.

monadic.title: Apply to k-cells
monadic.desc: f⍤k applies f to every k-cell of the right argument and reassembles the results. +/⍤1 on a matrix sums every row.

monadic.title: Modify at indices
monadic.desc: (f@I) X applies f to the elements of X at the positions named by I, leaving the rest unchanged.

monadic.title: Inverse
monadic.desc: Returns the reciprocal of a scalar or the inverse of a 2×2 matrix.

monadic.title: Identity
monadic.desc: Returns X unchanged.

dyadic.title: Left
dyadic.desc: Returns A and discards B.

monadic.title: Identity
monadic.desc: Returns X unchanged.

dyadic.title: Right
dyadic.desc: Returns B and discards A.

dyadic.title: Assignment
dyadic.desc: name ← value binds the right-hand side to the name on the left. It works for arrays and for function aliases like sum ← +/.

monadic.title: Diamond
monadic.desc: Separates two statements written on the same line.

monadic.title: Lamp
monadic.desc: Everything from ⍝ to the end of the line is ignored.

monadic.title: Right argument of a dfn
monadic.desc: Inside braces, ⍵ refers to the value passed on the right.

dyadic.title: Left argument of a dfn
dyadic.desc: Inside braces, ⍺ refers to the value passed on the left. Only valid when the dfn is called dyadically.

monadic.title: Negative literal
monadic.desc: Used inside numeric literals to mark negative values. It is part of the number, not a subtraction.

monadic.title: Open a dfn
monadic.desc: Begins an anonymous function. Inside, ⍵ is the right argument and ⍺ is the left.

monadic.title: Close a dfn
monadic.desc: Closes the body of an anonymous function.

monadic.title: Character literal
monadic.desc: Single quotes delimit a string. Double the quote inside a string to include one literal apostrophe.

monadic.title: System name
monadic.desc: Prefix for built-in system names. ⎕IO is the index origin (1). ⎕A is the uppercase alphabet. ⎕D is the digit string. ⎕TS is the current timestamp.

monadic.title: Identifier character
monadic.desc: Underscore is allowed inside variable names. It is not a primitive.

monadic.title: Function definition block
monadic.desc: Wraps a single function-defining statement between two ∇ markers. Example: ∇ inc ← {⍵+1} ∇ binds inc to the squared function. Inside the block any normal APL expression is allowed.

monadic.title: Identifier character
monadic.desc: Delta is allowed inside variable names, by convention used for changes or deltas.

monadic.title: Execute text as APL
monadic.desc: Takes a character vector, parses it and runs it as APL, returning the value of the last statement.

monadic.title: Empty numeric vector
monadic.desc: ⍬ is the canonical empty numeric vector: shape 0, rank 1. Common as a starting accumulator and as the result of reductions over empty inputs.

monadic.title: Character console I/O
monadic.desc: Standard character console input/output. In this interpreter ⍞ reads as an empty character vector since no terminal stream is connected.
